headline: CI gates for CHANGELOG drift + release-notes rollup
summary: The structured-changelog scripts shipped in v1.11.7 had no CI enforcement — an operator could land a `release-notes/v<X>.json` edit without running `--rebuild`, and the drift wouldn't surface until the next release ran the generator. The Docker workflow's lint job now runs `generate-changelog-entry.js --check`, `consolidate-release-notes.js --check`, and the existing `check-changelog-extract.js` on every push, so the gates fail loud at lint time instead of mid-release. Adds `consolidate-release-notes.js` to the maintainer-side tool set.

items.title: CHANGELOG drift gate in CI
items.body: `node scripts/generate-changelog-entry.js --check` runs in the docker-publish lint job. The gate does an in-memory rebuild of `CHANGELOG.md` from `release-notes/` and refuses the build when the on-disk file drifts from the JSON source. Forgotten `--rebuild` after a release-notes edit fails CI before any image build runs.

items.title: release-notes rollup gate in CI
items.body: `node scripts/consolidate-release-notes.js --check` runs in the same lint job and refuses the build when any non-current minor line still has per-patch files. Catches a missed consolidation between minor lines (the operator should run `--prune` when starting a new minor).

items.title: `scripts/consolidate-release-notes.js` maintainer tool
items.body: Rolls up `release-notes/v<X>.<Y>.<Z>.json` files for any minor line other than the current one (derived from `package.json#version`) into a single `release-notes/v<X>.<Y>.x.json` rollup. `--prune` deletes per-patch files after writing the rollup; the generator's lookup falls back to the rollup when per-patch files are absent. Run between releases only — never mid-release-flow, since reorganizing `release-notes/` would shift the tarball file set after the SHA-256 / SHA3-512 / ML-DSA digests were computed.
